displayName: First Notice of Loss / Notice of Claim Letter (PI Carrier)
template:
**DOCUMENT FORMAT â€” FIRST NOTICE OF LOSS / NOTICE OF CLAIM LETTER**:
A brief, factually conservative notice letter sent to an insurance carrier within the prompt-notice window after a covered loss. Establishes representation, places the carrier on notice, and triggers the carrier's duties of acknowledgment, investigation, and reasonable offer under the NAIC Unfair Claims Settlement Practices Act model. Target length: 1â€“2 pages.

**WHAT THIS TEMPLATE IS â€” AND WHAT IT IS NOT**:
This is a FNOL (First Notice of Loss) to a private insurance carrier â€” defendant's liability carrier OR client's own first-party carrier (PIP / UM / UIM / MedPay / homeowners). It is NOT a Tort Claims Act notice (which is a separately-prescribed government-entity notice with strict statutory contents and presentment deadlines â€” see future `tort_claims_act_notice_*` templates per state). Aliases for this template MUST NOT include `tca_notice` or `government_claim_notice` to prevent misrouting.

**DYNAMIC BEHAVIOR â€” READ documentOptions**:
- `recipientType`: `defendant_carrier` (default â€” at-fault driver/insured's liability carrier) | `client_carrier_first_party` (client's own carrier for PIP / MedPay) | `clients_uim_carrier` (client's UM/UIM carrier â€” preserves UIM rights)
- `subPractice`: `mva` (default) | `premises` | `product_liability` | (omit for general)
- `policyTypes`: array â€” `auto_liability`, `umbrella`, `UIM`, `UM`, `MedPay`, `PIP`, `homeowners`, `commercial_liability`
- `includeRequestForCoverageDeclaration`: boolean (default true)
- `requestPolicyLimitsDisclosure`: boolean (default true) â€” relevant only when `recipientType=defendant_carrier`
- `includePIPApplication`: boolean (default false; relevant only when `recipientType=client_carrier_first_party`)
- `damagesPreliminary`: boolean (default true at FNOL â€” full damages typically unknown)
- `clientStatus`: `driver` | `passenger` | `pedestrian` | `bicyclist` | `invitee` | `licensee` | `other`

**STRUCTURE** (follow line by line):

1. Date line.
2. VIA line (bold) â€” `**VIA Certified Mail â€” Return Receipt Requested AND Email**` for FNOL (creates a record of prompt notice).
3. Recipient block â€” carrier name; Claims Department; address; adjuster name + ID if known.
4. Re: block (multi-line, aligned labels):
   Re:    Our Client:           [client name]
          [If defendant_carrier] Your Insured: [defendant name]
          Date of Loss:         [date of incident]
          Loss Location:        [street / city / state]
          Claim No.:            [claim number, if assigned; else 'To Be Assigned']
5. Greeting â€” `Dear Claims Manager:` (institutional) or `Dear [Adjuster name]:` if known.

**BODY SECTIONS** (in order):

### Section 1 â€” Notice of Representation
One or two sentences identifying the firm and stating that all communications regarding this loss must be directed to the undersigned. Reference the firm's representation agreement on file (do not attach unless requested).

### Section 2 â€” Description of Loss (Preliminary)
One paragraph stating the date, time, and location of the loss; the mechanism (preliminary â€” reserve right to supplement); and `clientStatus` (driver / passenger / pedestrian / bicyclist / invitee / licensee). Adapt the framing by `subPractice`:
- `mva`: identify direction of travel, point of impact, and police-report number.
- `premises`: identify the dangerous condition and the premises (owner/occupant) by name.
- `product_liability`: identify the product, model, and the failure mode.
Keep the description factual and conservative â€” this letter creates an evidentiary record. State explicitly: 'This description is preliminary and based on currently available information; we reserve the right to supplement.'

### Section 3 â€” Identification of Injuries (Preliminary)
One paragraph: client sustained personal injuries requiring ongoing medical treatment. Treatment is ongoing and damages cannot be fully evaluated at this time. State explicitly: 'Damages are preliminary and reserved.'

### Section 4 â€” Coverage Requests [adapt by recipientType]

*(when recipientType = defendant_carrier)*
One paragraph asking the carrier to:
- Acknowledge receipt of this notice within the time required by the NAIC Unfair Claims Settlement Practices Act model (typically 15 days) and applicable state law;
- Confirm coverage and policy limits in writing (when `requestPolicyLimitsDisclosure=true`, cite the policy-limits-disclosure statute via JURISDICTION CONTEXT, else `[STATE POLICY LIMITS DISCLOSURE STATUTE]`);
- Identify all available policies (primary, excess, umbrella) covering the insured for this loss;
- Identify the assigned adjuster, claim number, and direct contact information;
- Direct all communications to undersigned counsel and not contact the client directly.

*(when recipientType = client_carrier_first_party OR clients_uim_carrier)*
One paragraph asking the carrier to:
- Acknowledge receipt of this notice;
- Confirm coverage and limits for any policy types listed in `policyTypes` (PIP / MedPay / UM / UIM / homeowners), citing the state PIP/UIM/no-fault statute via JURISDICTION CONTEXT (else `[STATE PIP/UIM STATUTE]`);
- Identify the adjuster and claim number;
- (when `clients_uim_carrier`) Confirm the carrier will not waive UIM rights or settle without conferring with this office.

### Section 5 â€” PIP / Benefits Application [include only when recipientType = client_carrier_first_party AND includePIPApplication = true]
One paragraph stating: 'Enclosed is the formal application for PIP / no-fault benefits under the applicable state statute (cite via JURISDICTION CONTEXT, else `[STATE PIP STATUTE]`). Medical authorizations and currently available bills/records will be submitted under separate cover. Please advise of any additional documentation required to process the application.'

### Section 6 â€” Preservation of Evidence (Cross-Reference)
One sentence: 'A separate preservation-of-evidence letter has been (or will be) sent to all parties having custody of relevant evidence, including incident-scene materials, photographs, surveillance, and statements. Please preserve all claim-file materials, statements, photographs, and reports relating to this loss.'

### Section 7 â€” Reservation of Rights and Closing
One short paragraph reserving all rights (additional damages, statutory remedies, litigation). Confirm that any direct contact with the client, contact with treating providers without authorization, or recorded statements will be considered communications to the firm and may be challenged. Provide direct line and email for follow-up.

Closing: `Very truly yours,` (suppress when stationery signature block is applied).

**CONSTRAINTS**:
- Do NOT make damages assertions beyond Section 3's preliminary statement â€” full damages belong in `demand_letter_pi`.
- Do NOT issue a time-limited demand here â€” that is `policy_limits_demand_pi` (or `policy_limits_demand_pi_tx`).
- Do NOT include any state-specific Tort Claims Act language â€” this letter is NOT a TCA notice. If the loss involves a public entity, prepare a separate TCA notice per the relevant state statute.
- Do NOT cite a specific state PIP/UIM/policy-limits statute unless the JURISDICTION CONTEXT supplies it â€” use `[STATE STATUTE CITATION]` placeholders.
- For any `policyTypes` not present, suppress that line entirely.
- If firm stationery is applied, SUPPRESS the `Very truly yours,` closing and signature block.
- Total body text: 300â€“700 words; target 1â€“2 pages.
hints: First Notice of Loss (FNOL) / Notice of Claim letter to a private insurance carrier. Adaptable by recipientType (defendant carrier / client first-party carrier / client UIM carrier). NOT a Tort Claims Act notice â€” TCA is a separate, statutorily-prescribed government-entity notice. Aliases must not include tca_notice or government_claim_notice. Cite NAIC UCSPA model for acknowledgment/investigation duties. Defer state-specific policy-limits disclosure / PIP / UM-UIM statutes to JURISDICTION CONTEXT. Damages assertions kept preliminary â€” full demand belongs in demand_letter_pi. 1â€“2 pages.

verificationSources.citation: NAIC Unfair Claims Settlement Practices Act (model)
ABA Model Rule 4.2 (Communication With Person Represented by Counsel)

knownLimitations: This is NOT a tort-claims-act notice â€” TCA notices have strict statutory content and presentment-deadline requirements that vary by state (e.g., M.G.L. c. 258 Â§ 4 in MA within 2 years; N.J.S.A. 59:8-8 in NJ within 90 days; O.C.G.A. Â§ 50-21-26 in GA within 12 months). Use state-specific TCA templates for public-entity claims.
Insurance 'prompt notice' requirements vary by policy and state â€” some states impose prejudice analysis on late notice (e.g., MA Augat); others enforce notice as a strict condition (e.g., FL).

Some carriers refuse policy-limits disclosure absent statutory trigger â€” state-specific FNOL siblings should cite the relevant state disclosure statute (e.g., M.G.L. c. 175 Â§ 112C in MA; Fla. Stat. Â§ 627.4137 in FL; O.C.G.A. Â§ 33-3-28 in GA).

PIP / no-fault application procedures vary widely by state â€” the client-carrier variant defers state-specific PIP rules to JURISDICTION CONTEXT, but form-based applications (e.g., MA PIP form) are NOT generated by this template.

FNOL is a notice instrument only â€” it does NOT preserve UIM rights against the client's own carrier in states requiring formal UIM consent-to-settle procedures (e.g., MA, NY); use a dedicated UIM-notice letter for those workflows.
